metadata.description: Nudgebee Azure Event Grid integration template. Deploys a System Topic (or reuses an existing one) and Event Subscription to stream real-time resource state changes to Nudgebee via webhook.
Your unique Nudgebee account token for secure event routing. Do not change or share this value.
Nudgebee webhook endpoint URL for receiving Event Grid events. This value is pre-filled when deployed via the Nudgebee dashboard.
Name for the Event Grid System Topic resource (used only when creating a new topic).
Name for the Event Grid Event Subscription. Leave as default — a unique suffix is appended automatically from the account token.
If your subscription already has an Event Grid System Topic for subscription-level events, provide its name here. Leave empty to auto-detect or create a new one.
Resource group of the existing system topic. Only required if existingSystemTopicName is provided and the topic is in a different resource group than this deployment.
Timestamp to force redeployment of deployment script.

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# === Path 1: Customer provided existing topic name ===
if [ -n "$EXISTING_TOPIC_NAME" ]; then
  echo "Using provided existing system topic: $EXISTING_TOPIC_NAME (RG: $EXISTING_TOPIC_RG)"
  jq -n --arg tn "$EXISTING_TOPIC_NAME" --argjson tc false \
    '{topicName: $tn, topicCreated: $tc}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH
  exit 0
fi

# === Path 2: Auto-detect and/or create ===
echo 'Detecting existing Event Grid System Topic...'

# Wait for EventGrid Contributor role to propagate (assigned via ARM resource)
echo 'Waiting for role assignment to propagate...'
ROLE_READY=false
for i in $(seq 1 18); do
  if az eventgrid system-topic list --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -o none 2>/dev/null; then
    ROLE_READY=true
    echo 'Role propagated successfully.'
    break
  fi
  echo "  Waiting... ($i/18)"
  sleep 10
done

if [ "$ROLE_READY" != "true" ]; then
  echo ''
  echo '========================================='
  echo 'ROLE ASSIGNMENT DID NOT PROPAGATE IN TIME'
  echo '========================================='
  echo 'The deploying user may not have permission to assign roles (Owner or User Access Administrator required).'
  echo ''
  echo 'Workaround: If your subscription already has an Event Grid System Topic, redeploy with:'
  echo '  existingSystemTopicName: <your-topic-name>'
  echo '  existingSystemTopicResourceGroup: <resource-group-of-topic> (if in a different RG)'
  echo ''
  echo 'Find your existing topic:'
  echo "  az eventgrid system-topic list --subscription $SUBSCRIPTION_ID --query \"[?properties.source=='/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID'].{name:name, resourceGroup:resourceGroup}\" -o table"
  echo '========================================='
  exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Check current resource group for existing system topic
if [ "$ROLE_READY" = "true" ]; then
  echo "Checking resource group '$RESOURCE_GROUP' for existing subscription-level system topic..."
  RG_TOPIC=$(az eventgrid system-topic list \
    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
    --query "[?properties.source=='/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID'].name | [0]" \
    -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true

  if [ -n "$RG_TOPIC" ] && [ "$RG_TOPIC" != "None" ] && [ "$RG_TOPIC" != "null" ]; then
    echo "Found existing system topic in current RG: $RG_TOPIC"
    jq -n --arg tn "$RG_TOPIC" --argjson tc false \
      '{topicName: $tn, topicCreated: $tc}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH
    exit 0
  fi
fi

# Step 2: Try subscription-level search (requires subscription-level read; may fail)
echo 'No topic in current RG. Trying subscription-level search...'
SUB_RESULT=$(az eventgrid system-topic list \
  --subscription "$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
  --query "[?properties.source=='/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID'].{name:name, rg:resourceGroup} | [0]" \
  -o json 2>/dev/null) || true

if [ -n "$SUB_RESULT" ] && [ "$SUB_RESULT" != "null" ]; then
  FOUND_NAME=$(echo "$SUB_RESULT" | jq -r '.name // empty')
  FOUND_RG=$(echo "$SUB_RESULT" | jq -r '.rg // empty')

  if [ -n "$FOUND_NAME" ]; then
    if [ "$FOUND_RG" = "$RESOURCE_GROUP" ]; then
      echo "Found existing system topic in current RG: $FOUND_NAME"
      jq -n --arg tn "$FOUND_NAME" --argjson tc false \
        '{topicName: $tn, topicCreated: $tc}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH
      exit 0
    else
      echo ''
      echo '========================================='
      echo 'EXISTING SYSTEM TOPIC IN ANOTHER RESOURCE GROUP'
      echo '========================================='
      echo "Topic name: $FOUND_NAME"
      echo "Resource group: $FOUND_RG"
      echo ''
      echo 'Please redeploy with these parameters:'
      echo "  existingSystemTopicName: $FOUND_NAME"
      echo "  existingSystemTopicResourceGroup: $FOUND_RG"
      echo '========================================='
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi

# Step 3: No existing topic found. Attempt to create one.
echo "No existing system topic found. Creating: $DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME"
if az eventgrid system-topic create \
  --name "$DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME" \
  --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
  --topic-type "Microsoft.Resources.Subscriptions" \
  --source "/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
  --location global 2>/tmp/create_error.txt; then
  echo "Successfully created system topic: $DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME"
  jq -n --arg tn "$DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME" --argjson tc true \
    '{topicName: $tn, topicCreated: $tc}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH
  exit 0
fi

CREATE_ERROR=$(cat /tmp/create_error.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 'Unknown error')
echo "Failed to create system topic: $CREATE_ERROR"

# Re-check current RG: a parallel deployment may have created the topic
RG_TOPIC=$(az eventgrid system-topic list \
  --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
  --query "[?properties.source=='/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID'].name | [0]" \
  -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true

if [ -n "$RG_TOPIC" ] && [ "$RG_TOPIC" != "None" ] && [ "$RG_TOPIC" != "null" ]; then
  echo "Found system topic after create conflict: $RG_TOPIC"
  jq -n --arg tn "$RG_TOPIC" --argjson tc false \
    '{topicName: $tn, topicCreated: $tc}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH
  exit 0
fi

# Check for name collision: desired name exists but with different source
CURRENT_NAME_SOURCE=$(az eventgrid system-topic show \
  --name "$DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME" \
  --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
  --query "properties.source" \
  -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true

if [ -n "$CURRENT_NAME_SOURCE" ] && [ "$CURRENT_NAME_SOURCE" != "/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" ]; then
  echo "A system topic named $DESIRED_TOPIC_NAME already exists in $RESOURCE_GROUP with a different source ($CURRENT_NAME_SOURCE). Choose a unique systemTopicName."
  exit 1
fi

if echo "$CREATE_ERROR" | grep -qi 'existing tracked system topic\|Conflict\|already exists'; then
  echo ''
  echo '========================================='
  echo 'EXISTING SYSTEM TOPIC IN ANOTHER RESOURCE GROUP'
  echo '========================================='
  echo 'A system topic already exists for this subscription in another resource group.'
  echo ''
  echo 'To resolve:'
  echo '  1. Find the existing topic:'
  echo "     az eventgrid system-topic list --subscription $SUBSCRIPTION_ID --query \"[?properties.source=='/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID']\" -o table"
  echo '  2. Redeploy with these parameters:'
  echo '     existingSystemTopicName: <name-from-step-1>'
  echo '     existingSystemTopicResourceGroup: <resource-group-from-step-1>'
  echo '========================================='
fi
exit 1

metadata.description: Name of the Event Grid System Topic (created or existing)
Resource ID of the Event Grid System Topic (created or existing)
Resource ID of the Event Subscription
Whether a new system topic was created (true) or existing was reused (false)
